prompt.role: Python development environment engineer working on macOS/Linux
prompt.task: Bootstrap the isolated Python working environment for the FlashcardQuizzer project and install every dependency declared in requirements.txt.

context.operating_system: macOS / Linux with zsh or bash

prompt.commands: - python3 -m venv venv - source venv/bin/activate - pip install --upgrade pip - pip install -r requirements.txt
prompt.success_criteria: - A ./venv directory exists at the project root and is activatable - pip is upgraded to the latest available release inside the venv - Every package listed in requirements.txt installs without errors

prompt.role: Senior Python engineer establishing a Definition of Done for the FlashcardQuizzer project
prompt.task: Wire up the project's Definition of Done so that completion is gated by three automated checks: test execution, code formatting, and static type checking.

context.existing_layout: utils/, tests/, and main.py at the project root
context.preferred_config_location: A single pyproject.toml at the project root

prompt.tools: - pytest: execute the unit test suite under ./tests and report coverage of ./utils - black: enforce a single canonical Python code style across the repository - mypy: perform static type checking on the production code in ./utils and main.py
prompt.success_criteria: - pytest runs the full suite and rejects coverage below 80% - black --check exits cleanly with zero files needing reformatting - mypy reports zero errors against utils/ and main.py - A single command runs the three checks sequentially and fails on the first failing step

prompt.role: Senior Python CLI engineer building the FlashcardQuizzer entry point
prompt.task: Refactor main.py into a CLI scaffold so that "python main.py --help" lists every available flag and "python main.py --mode sequential --file data/glossary.json" loads the glossary without crashing.

context.application: FlashcardQuizzer — a terminal flashcard quiz tool
context.starting_point: main.py is currently a TaskManager demo and must be replaced by the CLI entry point
context.data_file: data/glossary.json — a JSON array of objects with string "front" and "back" fields

prompt.requirements: - Expose --help (and -h) listing every flag with its purpose and default value - Expose --file as a required path that must point to an existing readable file - Expose --mode as a choice of sequential, random, adaptive — default sequential — where adaptive is described as prioritizing cards the user gets wrong - Validate the glossary JSON: top-level array of objects, each with string "front" and "back" fields, and reject an empty list - Report how many cards were loaded and which mode was selected, then exit cleanly - Leave the actual interactive quiz loop for a later iteration and print a clear "not yet implemented" notice

constraints.dependencies: Use the click package already declared in requirements.txt; no new dependencies
constraints.scope: Do not delete utils/task_manager.py or utils/file_handler.py — they remain available for later iterations

prompt.success_criteria: - python main.py --help prints all three flags with descriptions and defaults - python main.py --mode sequential --file data/glossary.json runs without crashing and reports the card count - An invalid or missing --file value errors out with a useful message - black --check main.py and mypy main.py both pass

prompt.role: Senior Python engineer designing the data ingestion layer for the FlashcardQuizzer application
prompt.task: Build a data-loading subsystem that reads a JSON glossary file, accepts two structural shapes, validates every card, and surfaces any failure as a user-friendly message instead of a Python traceback.

context.entry_point: main.py wraps the loader inside a click command and is responsible for printing the friendly message and exiting non-zero
context.module_location: utils/data_loader.py — new module, separated from the CLI layer per separation of concerns
context.accepted_shapes: - Array format: a top-level JSON array of card objects - Object format: a top-level JSON object wrapping a "cards" array
context.card_shape: Each card is a JSON object with non-empty string "front" and "back" fields

requirements.functional_requirements: - Read and parse the JSON file at the given path - Accept either the array format or the object-wrapper format transparently - Reject empty card lists with a clear message - Validate every card has non-empty string "front" and "back" fields - Return a list of immutable Flashcard records preserving file order
requirements.error_handling_requirements: - Missing file, permission errors, and other I/O failures must produce a friendly message - Malformed JSON must include the line and column from the parser - Per-card validation errors must include the 1-based card index in the message - main.py must catch the loader's exception, print "Error: <message>" to stderr, and exit with code 1 — never a Python traceback

constraints.architecture_principles: Separation of concerns: I/O parsing and validation live in utils/data_loader.py; the CLI in main.py only orchestrates and reports
constraints.dependencies: Standard library only for the loader (json, dataclasses, pathlib); no new third-party packages
constraints.error_surface: Expose a single FlashcardDataError exception so callers have one type to catch

prompt.class_under_test: utils.data_loader.load_flashcards and the FlashcardDataError it raises
prompt.test_categories: - Happy paths: array format, object format, multi-card ordering, immutability of returned cards - I/O errors: missing file, malformed JSON, unreadable file (POSIX only) - Top-level shape errors: wrong type at root, object missing the cards field, cards not an array, empty list in either format - Per-card shape errors: card not an object, missing front/back, non-string front/back, blank-string front/back, error message reports correct 1-based index for non-first card
prompt.success_criteria: - python main.py --file data/glossary.json (array format) loads cards and exits 0 - python main.py --file data/glossary_object.json (object format) loads cards and exits 0 - Malformed or invalid input produces a single-line friendly message on stderr and exit code 1, with no traceback - pytest suite passes with overall coverage at or above 80% - black --check and mypy both pass on the new and modified files

prompt.role: Senior Python engineer applying behavioral and creational design patterns to the FlashcardQuizzer quiz engine
prompt.task: Implement the quiz engine using the Strategy Pattern, exposing three interchangeable ordering strategies, and select the active strategy at runtime through a Factory Pattern driven by the existing --mode CLI flag.

context.current_architecture: main.py loads cards through utils.data_loader.load_flashcards and currently only prints the loaded card count and selected mode
context.integration_points: main.py passes the validated Flashcard list and the --mode string ("sequential" | "random" | "adaptive") into the new engine
context.data_model: Flashcard is a frozen dataclass with non-empty string "front" and "back" fields, defined in utils/data_loader.py

requirements.functional_requirements: - Define a QuizMode abstract base class that exposes next_card() returning the next Flashcard or None when the session ends, plus an overridable record_result(correct: bool) hook for strategies that adapt - Implement SequentialMode: serve cards in the original file order (1, 2, 3, ...) and stop after the last card - Implement RandomMode: serve every card exactly once in a shuffled order; accept an injectable random.Random instance to enable deterministic tests - Implement AdaptiveMode: serve every card once on the first pass, then re-serve missed cards ordered by miss count (highest first, original index breaks ties), until every card has been answered correctly at least once - Provide a create_quiz_mode factory that maps a case-insensitive, whitespace-tolerant mode name to the matching concrete strategy and raises a friendly error for any unknown name - Wire main.py to instantiate the active QuizMode through the factory rather than inspecting the mode string directly
requirements.extensibility_requirements: - Adding a fourth mode must only require a new QuizMode subclass plus a single dispatch entry in the factory; no caller in main.py should need to change

constraints.architecture_principles: Strategy Pattern for the three modes, Factory Pattern for construction; keep main.py decoupled from the concrete strategy classes
constraints.module_layout: Strategies live in utils/quiz_modes.py and the factory in utils/quiz_factory.py to keep the two patterns visibly separated
constraints.dependencies: Standard library only (abc, collections, random, typing); no new third-party packages

prompt.test_categories: - QuizMode base class: empty card list rejected, total property correct, abstract instantiation blocked, defensive copy of the input list - SequentialMode: original-order traversal, None after exhaustion, record_result is a no-op that does not change ordering - RandomMode: every card served exactly once, None after exhaustion, deterministic output with a seeded random.Random, observable shuffling versus the input order, works without an explicit rng - AdaptiveMode: first pass serves every card in order, all-correct ends after one pass, a missed card is re-served until mastered, miss-count prioritization with original-index tiebreaker, record_result before next_card is a no-op, a mastered card is never re-served - Factory: returns the correct concrete type per mode name, case-insensitive, whitespace-tolerant, rng is forwarded to RandomMode, rng is accepted but ignored by other modes, unknown mode raises a ValueError subclass, empty card list propagates the base class ValueError
prompt.success_criteria: - python main.py --file data/glossary.json --mode adaptive (and sequential, random) runs without crashing and reports the card count and selected mode - The full pytest suite passes with overall coverage at or above 80% - main.py contains no per-mode branching — it only calls create_quiz_mode and consumes the returned QuizMode

prompt.role: Senior Python engineer building a polished terminal user interface for the FlashcardQuizzer quiz engine
prompt.task: Implement the interactive CLI layer that drives the existing QuizMode strategies, replacing the placeholder click-based entry point with an argparse-driven flow that supports colored feedback and graceful exit handling.

context.current_state: main.py uses click and only prints the loaded card count; QuizMode strategies (sequential, random, adaptive) and the create_quiz_mode factory are already implemented in utils/quiz_modes.py and utils/quiz_factory.py
context.integration_points: main.py wires utils.data_loader.load_flashcards, utils.quiz_factory.create_quiz_mode, and the new interactive session loop into a single end-to-end flow

requirements.functional_requirements: - Replace click with argparse and expose -f / --file (required path), -m / --mode (sequential | random | adaptive, default sequential), and --stats (boolean flag) plus a --no-color escape hatch - Drive a per-card prompt loop that serves cards via QuizMode.next_card, normalizes the user's answer (lowercase, whitespace-collapsed), reports correctness back to the strategy via record_result, and accumulates per-session statistics - Render correct answers in ANSI green and incorrect answers in ANSI red, with auto-detection that disables color on non-TTY streams and when NO_COLOR is set in the environment - Accept "exit", "quit", or ":q" at any prompt to leave the session immediately; treat EOF (Ctrl+D) and SIGINT (Ctrl+C) the same way, with no traceback reaching the user - Print an end-of-session summary with totals and accuracy; when --stats is supplied, append a per-card breakdown showing correct and incorrect counts per question
requirements.extensibility_requirements: - Keep ANSI helpers, the session loop, and the entry point in separate modules so future UIs (e.g. a TUI or a web layer) can reuse the loop without inheriting argparse coupling

constraints.architecture_principles: Separation of concerns between argument parsing (main.py), terminal rendering (utils/colors.py), and the interactive loop with stats (utils/quiz_session.py)
constraints.dependencies: Standard library only — argparse, dataclasses, typing; no colorama or click in the new code path
constraints.ux: The CLI must never print a Python traceback on user-driven exit conditions (exit command, Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D, missing or malformed input file)

prompt.test_categories: - Colors module: ANSI wrapping on, no-op when disabled, NO_COLOR environment variable honored, non-TTY streams auto-disable colors - Answer normalization and is_correct: case-insensitive matching, whitespace tolerance, punctuation remains significant - SessionStats: empty-session defaults, per-card accumulation, accuracy ratio - run_session loop: records correct and incorrect answers, exits on each EXIT_COMMANDS member, exits gracefully on KeyboardInterrupt and EOFError, completes naturally when the deck is exhausted, emits ANSI escapes only when color is enabled - render_summary: empty-session message, basic totals without --stats, per-card breakdown when --stats is on - argparse parser: short and long flag forms, required -f, rejection of unknown modes, default values for --mode, --stats, and color - main() integration: zero exit code on success, --stats reaches the summary, FlashcardDataError surfaces a friendly stderr message with exit code 1, KeyboardInterrupt is swallowed and exits cleanly
prompt.success_criteria: - python main.py -f data/glossary.json -m sequential runs interactively, displays green or red feedback per answer, and prints a summary on completion - Typing "exit" at any prompt terminates the session and prints a final summary with no traceback - Sending Ctrl+C at any prompt exits with code 0 and a single friendly message, no Python traceback - python main.py -f data/glossary.json --stats appends a per-card breakdown to the summary - python main.py -f path/that/does/not/exist.json prints a single-line error to stderr and exits with code 1 - The full pytest suite passes with overall coverage at or above 80%

prompt.role: Student aligning the FlashcardQuizzer pytest suite with the rubric-mandated file and function names while keeping coverage above 80%
prompt.task: Reorganize the existing tests so the rubric-required filenames and test functions exist verbatim, and confirm the full suite still passes pytest --cov=. --cov-report=html above the 80% threshold.

context.starting_point: tests/ already contains test_data_loader.py, test_quiz_modes.py, test_quiz_factory.py, test_quiz_session.py, test_main_cli.py and helper module tests; baseline run is 102 passed at 97.80% coverage
context.rubric_mapping: - test_flashcard_loader.py must define test_load_valid_flashcards_array, test_load_invalid_json, and test_load_missing_required_field - test_quiz_modes.py must define test_quiz_mode_factory and test_adaptive_mode_behavior - test_integration.py must define test_full_session that simulates a user answering 3 questions and checks the final stats calculation

requirements.functional_requirements: - Rename tests/test_data_loader.py to tests/test_flashcard_loader.py and add the three rubric-named tests at the top of the file, keeping the existing loader tests for extra coverage - Add test_quiz_mode_factory (verifies create_quiz_mode returns SequentialMode, RandomMode, AdaptiveMode for the matching mode names) and test_adaptive_mode_behavior (verifies AdaptiveMode re-serves missed cards until mastered while not re-serving already-correct cards) to tests/test_quiz_modes.py - Create tests/test_integration.py with test_full_session that writes a 3-card glossary to tmp_path, loads it with load_flashcards, builds a SequentialMode via create_quiz_mode, drives run_session with a scripted input_fn producing 2 correct and 1 incorrect answer, and asserts on stats.correct, stats.incorrect, stats.accuracy, stats.per_card, and the render_summary output
requirements.non_functional_requirements: - Preserve the existing test organization and docstring style - Do not introduce new runtime dependencies; rely on pytest, tmp_path, io.StringIO, and the project's own modules

prompt.success_criteria: - pytest --cov=. --cov-report=html reports all tests passing with total coverage at or above 80% - pytest -v selects all six rubric-named tests by their exact qualified names and they all pass - htmlcov/index.html is regenerated for review
